questions.question: Explain the functions of the Network Layer in the OSI model.
keyPoints: Provides logical addressing using IP addresses
Performs routing - determines best path for data packets
Handles packet forwarding
Performs fragmentation if needed
Uses protocols like IP, ICMP, OSPF

questions.question: Differentiate between TCP and UDP protocols.
keyPoints: TCP: Connection-oriented, reliable, ordered delivery
UDP: Connectionless, unreliable, no order guarantee
TCP provides flow control and error recovery; UDP does not
TCP is slower; UDP is faster
TCP used for web, email; UDP for streaming, VoIP

questions.question: What is the purpose of the Presentation layer? Give examples of its functions.
keyPoints: Data translation between different systems
Encryption and decryption for security
Data compression to reduce bandwidth
Format conversion (JPEG, GIF, SSL/TLS)
Character code translation (ASCII, EBCDIC)

questions.question: Describe the process of encapsulation in the OSI model.
keyPoints: Each layer adds its header to data (encapsulation)
Application data becomes Segment (Transport)
Segment becomes Packet (Network)
Packet becomes Frame (Data Link)
Frame becomes Bits (Physical) at sender; reverse at receiver

questions.question: List all devices that operate at each of the first three OSI layers with brief justification.
keyPoints: Physical Layer: Hub, Repeater - transmit/receive raw bits
Data Link Layer: Switch, Bridge - use MAC addresses to forward frames
Network Layer: Router - use IP addresses to route packets

questions.question: Compare the OSI and TCP/IP reference models.
keyPoints: OSI has 7 layers; TCP/IP has 4 layers
OSI is a theoretical reference model; TCP/IP is practical implementation
OSI clearly separates functions; TCP/IP combines some layers
TCP/IP protocols are actual standards used in internet
OSI: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, Application
TCP/IP: Network Access, Internet, Transport, Application

questions.question: What is a Protocol Data Unit (PDU)? Name the PDU at each OSI layer.
keyPoints: PDU is the unit of data exchanged at each layer
Application/Presentation/Session: Data (or Message)
Transport: Segment (TCP) / Datagram (UDP)
Network: Packet
Data Link: Frame
Physical: Bits (or Signal)

questions.question: Draw a diagram showing the OSI 7-layer model with TCP/IP 4-layer model mapping. Explain the correspondence between layers with at least two protocols at each TCP/IP layer.
keyPoints: Diagram showing 7 OSI layers on left, 4 TCP/IP on right with mapping lines
Physical + Data Link = Network Access Layer (Ethernet, Wi-Fi)
Network = Internet Layer (IP, ICMP, IGMP)
Transport = Transport Layer (TCP, UDP)
Session + Presentation + Application = Application Layer (HTTP, FTP, SMTP, DNS, Telnet)
Detailed explanation of at least 2 protocols per TCP/IP layer with their functions

questions.question: Explain the functions of all seven layers of the OSI model in detail with one example protocol at each layer.
keyPoints: Physical: Bit transmission (Ethernet cables, hubs)
Data Link: Node-to-node transfer, MAC addressing (Ethernet, PPP)
Network: Routing, logical addressing (IP, OSPF)
Transport: End-to-end communication (TCP, UDP)
Session: Session management (RPC, NetBIOS)
Presentation: Data format, encryption (SSL/TLS, JPEG)
Application: User interface (HTTP, FTP, SMTP, DNS)
One real-world protocol example for each layer with explanation

questions.question: With the help of a diagram, explain the data encapsulation and decapsulation process when a user sends an email.
keyPoints: Diagram showing data flow from Application to Physical with headers added
Step-by-step: Data created at Application layer
Transport adds TCP/UDP header (Segment)
Network adds IP header (Packet)
Data Link adds MAC addresses (Frame)
Physical converts to bits for transmission
Decapsulation at receiver in reverse order
Role of each header in delivery explained

questions.question: A computer has IP address 192.168.1.100 and needs to send data to a server at 8.8.8.8. Explain how the TCP/IP model layers work together to accomplish this communication.
keyPoints: Application layer: User data (email/web request) created
Transport layer: Assigns source port, uses destination port (e.g., 80 for HTTP), adds sequence numbers for TCP
Internet layer: Adds source IP (192.168.1.100) and destination IP (8.8.8.8), handles routing
Network Access layer: Adds MAC addresses for local network delivery
Physical transmission and reversal at destination with explanation of encapsulation/decapsulation
How routers use IP to forward packets across networks
